generated_assessment.reasoning: This highly open (81st pct) and emotionally stable (18th pct neuroticism) female exhibits a baseline score of -237, already aligned with the adversarial goal. The T1 atomic fear appeal targeting trust in journalism is low-tier and short-duration; it primarily leverages threat salience and elite distrust cues. Her very low anger/hostility (2nd pct), high conscientiousness (deliberation at 74th pct), and ambivert extraversion make her less reactive to raw fear appeals, but her high openness to actions and feelings provides some surface for the confirmatory loss-framing to incrementally deepen her existing skepticism. Given the brief exposure and her stable, deliberative disposition, only a small movement toward the goal is realistic.

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Emotional_And_Moral_Engineering > Fear_Appeals > Personal_Safety_Fear_Appeal

attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Trust In Mainstream Journalism — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: information integrity and platforms, related policy debates, and societal impacts
